text: The patient is positioned in a modified lithotomy position under general anesthesia, with preoperative findings indicating an anterior defect in the external anal sphincter.
The surgeon removes the cetum after local marking with epinephrine injection, revealing the underlying tissue.

The surgeon opens the skin and mobilizes the sphincter complex on both sides, dividing it across the fistula. Dissection proceeds along the perineal body, excising the fistula tract along the vagina until clean and healthy tissue is visible.

The surgeon performs a layered closure, first closing the rectum, followed by the vagina, and placing intervening sutures between the levator muscles. The mobilized C-factor is overlapped and sutured using two O-PDA sutures, with a total of four sutures placed.

The surgeon closes the rectal and vaginal mucosa with two O-vital sutures. A JP drain is placed in the center of the wound and exits through a small stab incision in the left groin area. The skin along the perineal body is closed using three polysorb sutures.
